segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: Hi Don – did you get the copy of the article on recycling footwear that I emailed you?
segments.zh: 嗨，唐——我发你那篇关于回收鞋子的文章收到了吗？

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: Yeah – it's here ...

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: I've had a look at it.

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: So do you think it's a good topic for our presentation?
segments.zh: 那你觉得这个主题适合我们的演示吗？

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: Well, before I started reading it, I thought recycling footwear, well, although it's quite interesting, perhaps there isn't enough to say about it, cos we put shoes in recycling bins, they go to charity shops and that's about it. ... but there's much more to it than that.
segments.zh: 嗯，在我开始读之前，我觉得回收鞋子虽然挺有意思，但可能没什么好说的，因为我们把鞋子放进回收箱，它们被送到慈善商店，就这样了……但其实远不止如此。

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: I realise that now and I'm keen to research the topic more.
segments.zh: 我现在也意识到了，而且我很想进一步研究这个话题。

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: That's great.

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: One of the things I didn't realise until I read the article was just how many pairs of trainers get recycled!
segments.zh: 我读文章之前不知道的一件事是，原来有那么多运动鞋被回收了！

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: Well, a lot of young people wear them all the time now.
segments.zh: 嗯，现在很多年轻人整天都穿运动鞋。它们比普通鞋子更受欢迎了。

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: They've become more popular than ordinary shoes.

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: I know.
segments.zh: 我知道。我猜它们很耐穿，但穿在校服上是不是有点太随意了？我觉得不太合适。

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: I guess they are very hard-wearing, but don't they look a bit casual for school uniform?

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: I don't think they're right for that.

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: Actually, I think some of them look quite smart on pupils ... better than a scruffy old pair of shoes.
segments.zh: 实际上，我觉得有些鞋子穿在学生身上挺精神的……比一双邋遢的旧鞋好多了。那你一般一双鞋穿很久吗？

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: So do you keep shoes a long time?

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。不过我确实喜欢穿旧鞋干脏活，比如擦自行车。我得承认，我回收过一些完好的鞋子，没过时也合脚，只是因为穿在我身上不好看了。这很糟糕，对吧？

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: Though I do tend to wear my old pairs for doing dirty jobs like cleaning my bike.

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: I must admit, I've recycled some perfectly good shoes, that haven't gone out of fashion and still fit, just because they don't look great on me any more.

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: That's awful isn't it?

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: I think it's common because there's so much choice.
segments.zh: 我觉得这很常见，因为选择太多了。那篇文章确实提到最近的鞋类销售额大幅增长。

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: The article did say that recent sales of footwear have increased enormously.

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: That didn't surprise me.

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: No. But then it said that the amount of recycled footwear has fallen: it's 6 percent now compared to a previous level of 11 percent.
segments.zh: 不惊讶。但文章说回收鞋类的比例下降了：现在是6%，之前是11%。这似乎说不通。

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: That doesn't seem to make sense.

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: That's because not everything goes through the recycling process.
segments.zh: 那是因为不是所有东西都经过回收过程。有些鞋子因为这样那样的原因不够好，无法转售，就被淘汰了。

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: Some footwear just isn't good enough to re-sell, for one reason or another, and gets rejected.

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: So let's find some examples in the article of footwear that was rejected for recycling.
segments.zh: 那我们就在文章里找一些被拒绝回收的鞋子的例子吧。

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。我记得在采访回收经理时有一些。嗯，找到了。

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: I think there are some in the interview with the recycling manager.

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: Yeah – here it is.

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。我们先从女士高跟鞋说起吧。他关于那些鞋说了什么？

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: Let's start with the ladies' high-heeled shoes.

segments.speaker: BELLA
segments.en: What did he say about those?

segments.speaker: DON
segments.en: He said they were probably expensive – the material was suede and they were beige in colour – it looked like someone had only worn them once, but in a very wet field so the heels were too stained with mud and grass to re-sell them.
segments.zh: 他说那些鞋可能很贵——材质是绒面革，颜色是米色——看起来只穿过一次，但在一片很湿的田地里，所以鞋跟沾满了泥和草，没法再卖了。

items.prompt: At first, Don thought the topic of recycling footwear might be too

pairs.note: 题干说‘可能太……’，原文说‘没有足够的内容’，意思相反但表达相同观点。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中‘too’表示‘太……’，原文中‘isn't enough’表示‘不够’，两者都暗示话题内容不足，通过这种反向表达定位。

items.prompt: When discussing trainers, Bella and Don disagree about

pairs.p: don't they look a bit casual for school uniform? I don't think they're right for that
pairs.note: 题干说'不同意关于...'，原文中Don用反问和否定表达反对意见，对应disagree。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Bella和Don在讨论训练鞋时不同意什么，定位句是Don说'它们看起来有点随意，不适合校服'，表明他不同意Bella的观点，因此答案B（是否适合校服）正确。

items.prompt: Bella says that she sometimes recycles shoes because

pairs.note: 原文中'recycled'与题干'recycles'是动词时态变化，但核心词相同。
'some'在这里表示'某些'，与'sometimes'（有时）意思相近，都表示非经常性。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Bella有时回收鞋子的原因，定位句提到她回收了一些完好的鞋子，因为看起来不再好看。通过'recycled'和'some'定位到原文。

items.prompt: What did the article say that confused Don?

pairs.note: confused 对应 doesn't seem to make sense，都是表示不理解。
it 指代前文提到的 article。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到 Don 说 'That doesn't seem to make sense'，与题干 confused 对应，且 it 指代 article。

pairs.note: 题干中的'high-heeled shoes'在原文中被简化为'heels'，指鞋跟部分，但实际指代整双高跟鞋。

paraphrase.explain: 通过'heels'定位到描述鞋子的句子，其中提到鞋跟沾满泥和草，无法转售，对应选项E。

items.prompt: Why did the project to make 'new' shoes out of old shoes fail?
Bella and Don agree that they can present their topic
